title: EXPLAINER: How do parties and states set presidential votes?
shortDescription: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Even before President Joe Biden told Democrats his preference for reordering the presidential primary calendar, states began balking.

title: Iowa Democratic Party chair won’t seek reelection
shortDescription: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — The head of the Iowa Democratic Party won't seek reelection in the wake of a vote by the national party to put the South Carolina primary ahead of Iowa's caucuses, which have long been the first presidential nominating contest in the country.

title: Jan. 6 panel pushes Trump’s prosecution in forceful finish
At Hanukkah reception, Biden to condemn rising antisemitism
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is condemning growing antisemitism in remarks for a Hanukkah reception at the White House that will include a menorah lighting and blessing.The Democratic president will tell guests at the Monday night event that silence is complicity, according to White House officials, and will add that it's imperative that hate, violence and antisemitism are condemned.

title: US homeless numbers stay about the same as before pandemic
shortDescription: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President Joe Biden's administration announced Monday that it is ramping up efforts to help house people now sleeping on sidewalks and in tents and cars as a new federal report confirms what's obvious to people in many cities: Homelessness is persisting despite increased local efforts.

title: McCarthy’s race for speaker risks upending House on Day One
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — In his quest to rise to House speaker, Kevin McCarthy is charging straight into history — potentially becoming the first nominee in 100 years unable to win the job on a first-round floor vote.

title: Biden marks 50th anniversary of death of wife, daughter
shortDescription: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — President Joe Biden and his family held a private memorial service Sunday to mark the 50th anniversary of the car crash that killed his first wife and their baby daughter.

title: ALS patients contend with $158K price tag on new drug
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — For two years, Becky Mourey pushed the Food and Drug Administration to approve an experimental drug for her Lou Gehrig’s disease.She went to members of Congress and health regulators to make the case for Relyvrio, until patient-advocates finally prevailed.

title: Vote counting finishes in Fiji election with no clear winner
shortDescription: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Vote counting finished in Fiji's general election Sunday but there was no clear winner, and various political parties are now negotiating to form a coalition government.

title: Low turnout in Tunisia parliament vote amid economic vows
shortDescription: TUNIS, Tunisia (AP) — Tunisians on Saturday voted to elect a new parliament, to the backdrop of a soaring cost-of-living crisis and concerns of democracy backsliding in the North African country — the cradle of Arab Spring protests a decade ago.

title: Oppenheimer wrongly stripped of security clearance, US says
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration has reversed a decades-old decision to revoke the security clearance of Robert Oppenheimer, the physicist called the father of the atomic bomb for his leading role in World War II’s Manhattan Project.

title: Peru’s president exhorts congress to approve early elections
shortDescription: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru’s caretaker president, Dina Boluarte, chastised lawmakers for not endorsing widespread calls for early elections as a way out of a deepening political crisis triggered when the ousted Pedro Castillo tried to shut down Congress.

title: Varadkar returns as Irish leader in political job-share deal
shortDescription: LONDON (AP) — Leo Varadkar returned for a second term as Ireland’s prime minister on Saturday as part of a job-sharing deal made by the country’s centrist coalition government.Lawmakers voted to approve Varadkar's nomination to replace Micheál Martin during a special session of the Dail, the lower house of Ireland’s parliament.

title: What Trump promised, Biden seeks to deliver in his own way
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump pledged to fix U.S. infrastructure as president.

title: Want to email your doctor? You may be charged for that
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The next time you message your doctor to ask about a pesky cough or an itchy rash, you may want to check your bank account first — you could get a bill for the question. Hospital systems around the country are rolling out fees for some messages that patients send to physicians, who they say are spending an increasing amount of time poring over online queries, some so complex that they require the level of medical expertise normally dispensed during an office visit.

title: Jan. 6 committee eyes referring criminal charges for Trump
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House panel investigating the Jan. 6 attack on the U.S. Capitol is considering recommending the Justice Department pursue an unprecedented criminal charge of insurrection and two other counts against former President Donald Trump.

title: Inside the ugly fight to become the next Republican chair
shortDescription: NEW YORK (AP) — Struggling to unify after another disappointing election, the Republican National Committee is consumed by an increasingly nasty leadership fight as the GOP navigates its delicate relationship with former President Donald Trump.

title: US buying 3M barrels of oil to start replenishing reserves
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Friday it is buying 3 million barrels of oil to begin to replenish U.S. strategic reserves that officials drained earlier this year in a bid to stop gasoline prices from rising amid production cuts by OPEC and a ban on Russian oil imports.

title: Oregon GOP state Sen. with stormy political career resigns
shortDescription: SALEM, Ore. (AP) — A Republican state senator who once led Oregon's GOP party, and who railed against pandemic safety measures, Democratic senators and even members of his own party, has abruptly resigned as a lawmaker.

title: Judge to halt provision making California gun suits costlier
shortDescription: SAN DIEGO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday said he will block a provision in a new California law to take effect next year that gun advocates argued was designed to hinder anyone from challenging the state's famously restrictive gun laws.

title: Panel assembled for hearing on heating aid proposal in Maine
shortDescription: Leaders of the Maine Legislature have assembled a temporary committee to convene a hearing on a $474 million emergency heating assistance proposal, a development that was hailed by the leader of Senate Republicans who rejected the first attempt at passage, officials said Friday.

title: QAnon follower who chased officer on Jan. 6 gets 5 years
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Iowa construction worker and QAnon follower was sentenced Friday to five years in prison for his role in the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol, when he led a crowd chasing a police officer who diverted rioters away from lawmakers.

title: Democrats set aside donations from ex-FTX CEO Bankman-Fried
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The three primary committees charged with electing Democrats said Friday that they would set aside more than $1 million in donations this election cycle from disgraced crypto mogul Samuel Bankman-Fried, who is accused of making tens of millions of dollars in illegal campaign donations.

title: Manchin bid to speed energy permits to reemerge in 2023
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — A push by West Virginia Sen. Joe Manchin to speed permits for natural gas pipelines and other energy projects has failed in the current Congress, but could come back as soon as next month.

title: Biden signs bill to keep government open through Dec. 23
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden signed legislation Friday to fund the government for an additional week as lawmakers race to finish work on a full-year spending package before they head home for the holidays and a new Congress is sworn in.

title: Trump omits dinner with white nationalist in speech to Jews
shortDescription: NEW YORK (AP) — Speaking at a conference of Orthodox Jews on Friday, Donald Trump did not address a widely criticized private meal he shared last month with a Holocaust-denying white nationalist and a rapper who has spewed antisemitic conspiracies.

title: Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen to visit sub-Saharan Africa
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen will travel in January to South Africa, Zambia, and Senegal, her office announced Friday, in what will be the first of a string of Biden administration official visits to sub-Saharan Africa next year.

title: Holcomb says he’s focused on last years as Indiana governor
shortDescription: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Indiana Gov. Eric Holcomb is entering his final two years in office, with several candidates already lining up to take over his Statehouse desk as term limits prevent him from seeking reelection again.

title: Biden urges veterans to seek health benefits under new law
shortDescription: NEW CASTLE, Del. (AP) — President Joe Biden urged military veterans on Friday to take advantage of new healthcare opportunities under legislation that he signed in August.

title: New Biden plan aims to cut homelessness 25% in 2 years
shortDescription: The White House is rolling out a nationwide push to reduce homelessness 25% in major cities that have long been struggling with the crisis.

title: Jan 6 committee: How to watch Monday hearing on Capitol attack
shortDescription: The final Jan. 6 committee hearing on the Capitol attack is Monday at 1 p.m. EST. Watch the hearing live at USA TODAY.

title: Congress in 11th hour push to fund government before GOP takes House
shortDescription: The 117th Congress is in session for one last week as lawmakers race to avert a government shutdown before Republicans take the House.

title: Biden to meet with Ecuador president on Monday. Here's what to know.
shortDescription: President Joe Biden is expected to meet with Ecuador President Guillermo Lasso. The two will discuss the Ukraine-Russia war and immigration.

title: Jan. 6 committee to weigh actions on GOP lawmakers who flouted subpoenas
shortDescription: The Jan. 6 committee has weighed options such as censures and referrals to the House Ethics Committee for lawmakers who ignored subpoenas.

title: GOP vs. ESG: Why DeSantis, Republicans are fighting 'woke' investing
shortDescription: Why red states are pulling billions in pension funds from BlackRock and piling on investigations: ESG or environmental, social, governance principles.

title: DOJ, public await Jan. 6 committee's Monday decision on Trump
shortDescription: The House Jan. 6 committee, after 18 months of investigating, is set to take its most defining actions in votes on criminal referrals Monday.

title: Kevin McCarthy faces GOP opposition in bid for Speaker from the House
shortDescription: Kevin McCarthy's bid to become the next Speaker of the House may involve a messy floor fight and a contested race as Republicans hold narrow control.

title: Jan. 6 panel to vote on urging DOJ to charge Donald Trump: reports
shortDescription: The House Jan. 6 committee will vote Monday on urging the Justice Department to charge Donald Trump related to the Capitol riot, reports said.

title: Meet the ex-federal judge who will monitor Trump Organization's finances
shortDescription: Jones' early legal career focused on fighting organized crime. While a federal trial court judge, she issued a major ruling on same-sex marriage.

title: This week: Government shutdown avoided, same-sex marriage law passes
shortDescription: After domestic spending debate held up negotiations, Congress passed a measure to delay the spending bill vote another week. Here's what else happened this week.

title: Trump's NFT collection draws ridicule from opponents and allies
shortDescription: Former President Donald Trump's announcement of his NFT "trading card collection" has drawn ridicule from both frequent opponents and allies.

title: Capitol rioter among first in building sentenced to 60 months in prison
shortDescription: An Iowa construction worker, Doug Jensen, is easily spotted in pictures from Jan. 6 thanks to the "Q" tee shirt he wore that day, an homage to QAnon.

title: Pelosi 'optimistic' about omnibus spending bill
shortDescription: Congress passed a short-term spending bill that will keep the government open as lawmakers negotiate the appropriations package for the fiscal year.

title: Can Biden best China for influence in Africa?
shortDescription: African leaders came to President Biden's summit to gauge Washington’s seriousness in becoming a more reliable partner than it’s been in the past.

title: A judge ruled Title 42 should end, but the Biden administration appealed
shortDescription: The Biden administration has found itself caught between conflicting federal court rulings on a Trump public health policy that impacts immigration.

title: Why some law schools are ditching the 'great' Chief Justice John Marshall
shortDescription: Two law schools have dropped Chief Justice John Marshall's name, citing his ownership of slaves and some of his Supreme Court opinions about slavery.

title: Government shutdown 2022: Senate extends federal funding to Dec. 23
shortDescription: If Congress can't agree on a massive spending bill, the issue is likely to come up early next year when Republicans will regain control of the House.

title: Senate sends $858 billion Defense spending bill to President Biden
shortDescription: The bill authorizes a 4.6% pay raise for military and civilian workers. It also sends $500 million more in aid to Ukraine than Biden had proposed.

title: What we know about the JFK files that Biden released Thursday
shortDescription: President Joe Biden ordered the release of JFK files Thursday, sourced from thousands of documents tied to Kennedy's death.

title: Congress Passes Bill to Rein in Conflicts of Interest for Consultants Such as McKinsey
shortDescription: President Joe Biden is expected to sign the legislation, whose Senate sponsors cited ProPublica’s reporting on McKinsey’s conflicts in working for both the FDA and opioid makers like Purdue Pharma.

title: Why Congress Can’t Stop the CIA From Working With Forces That Commit Abuses
shortDescription: The Leahy Law prohibits the U.S. military from providing training and equipment to foreign security forces that commit human rights abuses, but it does not apply to U.S. intelligence agencies. Vermont Sen. Patrick Leahy said it should.

title: Conservative Activist Poured Millions Into Groups Seeking to Influence Supreme Court
shortDescription: Newly obtained records show how Leonard Leo, an architect of the right-wing takeover of the courts, has been funding groups pushing to change elections and anti-discrimination laws.

title: Why the U.S. Is Losing the Fight to Ban Toxic Chemicals
shortDescription: From a powerful chemical industry that helped write the toxic substances law to an underfunded EPA lacking in resolve, the flaws in the American chemical regulatory apparatus run deep.

title: Hedge Fund Manager Ken Griffin Sues IRS Over “Unlawful Disclosure” of His Tax Information to ProPublica
shortDescription: The Citadel founder was among dozens of ultrawealthy Americans spotlighted in our Secret IRS Files series, which used a trove of agency data to reveal how billionaires avoid paying taxes and use their money to influence tax policy.

title: Congress Passed an $858 Billion Military Bill. Here’s What’s in It.
shortDescription: Lawmakers approved an 8 percent overall budget increase, funding for Taiwan and Ukraine, and a slew of provisions focused on judicial security and environmental protections.By Catie Edmondson

title: Congress Inches Toward Spending Deal, Clearing a Weeklong Extension
shortDescription: The Senate passed legislation to avert a government shutdown this weekend as negotiators worked to finalize an agreement to provide funding through September.By Emily Cochrane

title: Congress Clears Military Bill Repealing Vaccine Mandate for Troops
shortDescription: Passage of the $858 billion bill by the Senate sends the annual military policy measure to President Biden’s desk.By Catie Edmondson

title: Senate clears one-week funding patch, averting government shutdown
shortDescription: The measure gives top appropriators more time to flesh out a $1.7 trillion year-end spending package that Congress is racing to clear before the holidays.
